strengths: Current Staff Engineer at re:cinq (hiring company) with active AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development—directly demonstrates required AI agent expertise and insider knowledge of technical direction.
14+ years full-stack delivery with TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB, MySQL, AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes, and established practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, microservices).
Proven architecture at scale: handled millions of requests/second (Dunnhumby), shipped top-10 ranked Apple TV app, designed open-source GIS platform, and mentored teams on engineering practices.
concerns: React expertise not highlighted; resume emphasizes Ember.js instead of the primary stack's frontend framework, though both share transferable modern framework concepts.
Nest.js and Vitest not explicitly mentioned despite strong Node.js and testing expertise (TDD, CI practices documented), leaving framework/tooling familiarity unclear.
Authentication, authorization, and security best-practices not explicitly detailed in employment history, though 14+ years of production experience suggests foundational knowledge.

key_factors.factor: Full-stack architectural capability and modern tooling depth

response.justification: Strong fit for senior fullstack role with 14+ years of proven full-stack architecture and delivery across web, mobile, and embedded systems. Currently a Staff Engineer at re:cinq working directly on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP frameworks, and developer tooling—directly addressing the explicit AI agent development requirement while providing insider knowledge of the organization's technical direction. Deep expertise in TypeScript, Node.js, multiple databases, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, and established engineering practices (TDD, microservices, scaling) aligns with core technical needs. React/Nest.js specificity gaps are offset by strong modern JavaScript foundation and demonstrated ability to ship high-impact products rapidly (top-10 Apple TV app, millions of processed mails annually, open-source platforms). Track record of mentoring and collaboration supports delivery orientation.
